# Contracts

## OpenAPI spec

The Federation Resource API spec is tracked as **`federation-v130.yaml`** (OpenAPI 3 YAML).  
`config/projects.yaml` → `federation.openapi_relative` points at this file.

## Autogenerated contract suite (GET)

Same pattern as DCC: discover → generate cases from the spec → run GETs → JSON + HTML reports.

```bash
# From repo root (venv active)
python -m framework.contract_runner.federation_cli

# Or
bash scripts/run_federation_contract_suite.sh
```

Options mirror DCC (`--tags`, `--no-negative`, `--strict-filter-data`, `--spec`, `--report`, `--base-url`).  
Reports default to **`reports/federation/contract/`** (override with `FEDERATION_CONTRACT_REPORT_DIR`).

**Base URL:** set `DATACOMNS_FEDERATION_BASE_URL` to the Federation **host** only (e.g. `https://federation-qa.ccdi.cancer.gov`); `api_prefix` `/api/v1` is appended from `config/projects.yaml`. The local web UI sets QA/stage/prod Federation hosts automatically for the Federation contract suite (not the DCC host).

**CPI / subject-mapping:** some gateways do not expose `GET /subject-mapping`. By default the generator skips that path. To include it, set `FEDERATION_CONTRACT_EXCLUDE_PATHS` to empty. To skip additional paths, use a comma-separated list of OpenAPI path keys (e.g. `/subject-mapping,/foo`).

## AI context (optional)

`framework.ai.context.build_project_context_bundle("federation")` reads `openapi_relative` when the file exists.

## Legacy note

Contract / schema pytest tests (`@pytest.mark.contract`) can also use an OpenAPI file placed here; update `openapi_relative` if you use a different filename.
